name: BBN/CMB bounds on MeV-scale thermal dark sectors
observable: light thermal relic impact on expansion and light-element/CMB observables
required_observables: dark-sector temperature ratio; thermalization history; abundance/energy density at BBN and recombination; annihilation or decay channels to SM/neutrinos/dark radiation
available_observables: m_WD 1sigma = 0.256-0.577 GeV; m_chi 1sigma = 0.0368-0.0892 GeV; portal_coupling = 0; Treh MAP = 0.0978 GeV
missing_observables: dark-sector temperature ratio; abundance/yield; branching fractions; entropy transfer; BBN/CMB likelihood mapping
tested_subregion: PTArcade 1sigma masses overlap MeV-GeV dark-sector cosmology, but published thermal-SM bounds do not apply without thermal coupling/temperature assumptions
source_title: Refined Bounds on MeV-scale Thermal Dark Sectors from BBN and the CMB

name: Updated BBN/CMB MeV dark-sector bounds after LUNA rate
required_observables: same as C01 plus nuclear-rate convention used in BBN likelihood
tested_subregion: Same mass subregion as C01; used as robustness/update source, not a separate exclusion
source_title: Addendum: Refined bounds on MeV-scale thermal dark sectors from BBN and the CMB

name: Delta N_eff / dark radiation from secluded sector
observable: extra relativistic energy density at BBN/CMB
required_observables: dark-sector temperature ratio; relativistic degrees of freedom at neutrino decoupling/recombination; entropy dumps from W_D/chi
available_observables: masses and FOPT temperatures available; no temperature ratio or relativistic-energy budget
missing_observables: xi=T_dark/T_SM; decoupling temperature; entropy evolution; Delta N_eff
tested_subregion: Relevant if the secluded sector was ever populated or has residual radiation; not testable from mass/coupling alone

name: BBN/CMB energy release from PTA-scale dark phase transition
observable: late energy injection / altered expansion from supercooled FOPT and reheating
required_observables: full thermal history; visible-sector reheating or decoupled-sector reheating; fraction of released energy into SM radiation vs dark radiation; decay lifetimes if any
available_observables: PTA MAP alpha is extremely large; Treh_MAP = 0.0978 GeV; no SM portal
missing_observables: energy-partition model; entropy injection; dark-sector temperature; BBN/CMB observables
tested_subregion: MAP context and 1sigma region have strong supercooling; cosmological viability cannot be inferred from GW fit alone
source_title: Does NANOGrav observe a dark sector phase transition?

name: Self-interacting dark matter limits from halo/cluster structure
observable: transfer/self-interaction cross section per mass sigma_T/m as a function of velocity
required_observables: stable DM fraction in W_D; non-Abelian vector self-scattering cross sections; velocity dependence at dwarf/cluster scales; relic abundance
missing_observables: sigma_T/m(v); relic abundance/fraction; dark bound-state/plasma effects if relevant
tested_subregion: Relevant only if W_D is cosmologically stable and abundant; no direct exclusion from mass/gD alone
source_title: Dark matter self-interactions and small scale structure

name: Bullet Cluster / merging-cluster self-interaction upper bounds
required_observables: same as C05 with cluster velocity mapping and halo-model assumptions
missing_observables: sigma/m at cluster velocities; DM fraction in W_D
tested_subregion: Overlaps if W_D is the DM component; requires computed cross section and abundance
source_title: Direct constraints on the dark matter self-interaction cross-section from the merging galaxy cluster 1E0657-56

name: Higgs-portal invisible/exotic Higgs decay constraints
observable: Higgs invisible or exotic branching fractions
required_observables: nonzero Higgs portal/mixing; decay widths h->dark states; branching fractions
available_observables: portal_coupling = 0 in minimal model; m_dark < m_h/2
missing_observables: none for minimal model; for extensions need portal coupling and widths
tested_subregion: Not applicable to the minimal secluded model; becomes relevant only in nonzero-portal extensions
source_title: The Higgs-portal for dark matter: effective field theories versus concrete realizations

name: Higgs-portal direct detection constraints
required_observables: nonzero portal/mixing; local DM fraction; elastic scattering matrix element
missing_observables: none for minimal model; for extensions need sigma_SI and relic fraction
tested_subregion: Not applicable with zero portal; mass alone cannot be compared to direct-detection bounds
source_title: The Higgs-portal for dark matter: effective field theories versus concrete realizations

name: Beam-dump/fixed-target dark-sector searches
observable: visible production and decay/scattering rates in fixed-target experiments
required_observables: SM portal such as kinetic mixing, scalar mixing, or other mediator; production cross section; decay modes/lifetimes
available_observables: No Abelian kinetic mixing for non-Abelian SU(2)_D before breaking; Higgs portal fixed to zero
missing_observables: none for minimal model; for extensions need portal parameters and detector-level rates
tested_subregion: Not applicable to secluded minimal model; MeV-GeV mass overlap alone is insufficient

name: Collider and accelerator portal searches for light hidden sectors
observable: portal-mediated production, missing energy, displaced or visible decays
required_observables: explicit SM portal; production/decay model; detector efficiencies
available_observables: portal_coupling = 0; no kinetic mixing; all new particles SM singlets
tested_subregion: Not applicable without an SM portal; community reports used to identify missing portal assumptions
source_title: US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017: Community Report

name: Thermalization / decoupling of secluded dark sector
observable: interaction rate Gamma/H and decoupling temperature
required_observables: initial condition; portal coupling if any; gravitational/other connectors; dark-sector self-thermalization; entropy transfer
available_observables: portal = 0; dark self-coupling gD 1sigma available; FOPT temperatures available
missing_observables: initial dark temperature; connector rate to SM; decoupling temperature
tested_subregion: Central missing calculation for interpreting all BBN/CMB/Neff constraints
source_title: Observational Constraints on Decoupled Hidden Sectors

name: Perturbativity and unitarity of dark SU(2) coupling
observable: coupling size and CW expansion validity
required_observables: gD posterior; perturbative criterion; FOPT backend validity flags
available_observables: gD 1sigma = 0.853-0.916; gD MAP = 0.863; beta_lambda and masses derived; PTArcade warns strong supercooling/template caveats
missing_observables: none for simple gD perturbativity; higher-loop/RG stability optional
tested_subregion: 1sigma gD is below sqrt(4*pi), so basic perturbativity is passed; no exclusion applied because this is a consistency check
